content.text:
Here is a valid sample data call to the manySchemasTool:

- string: "Hello, world!"
- stringMin: "Hello!"
- stringMax: "ShortText"
- stringEmail: "sample@example.com"
- stringEmoji: "😊"
- stringUrl: "https://example.com"
- stringUuid: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"
- stringCuid: "cjojsnvz50000q5zs5j0h7s3r"
- stringRegex: "test-1234"
- number: 42
- numberGt: 5
- numberLt: 4
- numberGte: 1
- numberLte: 1
- numberMultipleOf: 8
- numberInt: 7
- exampleArray: ["one", "two", "three"]
- arrayMin: ["item1"]
- arrayMax: ["a", "b", "c"]
- date: "2023-05-21T14:00:00Z"
- dateAfter: "2024-03-01T10:30:00Z"
- dateBefore: "2024-05-13T09:00:00Z"
- actualData: "2024-06-12T15:00:00Z"
- object: {"foo": "bar", "bar": 123}
- objectNested: {"user": {"name": "Al", "age": 25}}
- objectPassthrough: {}
- objectLoose: {}
- optional: null
- nullable: null
- enum: "A"
- nativeEnum: "B"
- unionPrimitives: "unionSample"
- unionObjects: {"amount": 27, "inventoryItemName": "pen"}
- default: "test"

This satisfies all required schema constraints!

content.text:
Here is an example call to the manySchemasTool tool with valid sample data:

- string: "Hello, world!"
- stringMin: "Hello"
- stringMax: "ShortText"
- stringEmail: "example@email.com"
- stringEmoji: "😄"
- stringUrl: "https://example.com"
- stringUuid: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"
- stringCuid: "cjnzpcl1e0000qzrmn831i7rn"
- stringRegex: "test-abc"
- number: 42
- numberGt: 4
- numberLt: 5
- numberGte: 1
- numberLte: 1
- numberMultipleOf: 6
- numberInt: 7
- exampleArray: ["apple", "banana", "orange"]
- arrayMin: ["one"]
- arrayMax: ["a", "b", "c"]
- date: "2024-06-10T15:00:00Z"
- dateAfter: "2024-06-11T00:00:00Z"
- dateBefore: "2024-06-09T23:59:59Z"
- actualData: "2024-06-10T12:00:00Z"
- object: { foo: "bar", bar: 123 }
- objectNested: { user: { name: "Jo", age: 18 } }
- objectPassthrough: {}
- objectLoose: {}
- optional: null
- nullable: null
- enum: "A"
- nativeEnum: "A"
- unionPrimitives: "union"
- unionObjects: { amount: 99, inventoryItemName: "Widget" }
- default: "test"

If you need explanations for each field or want to see variations, just let me know!
